title: Plan a Stage 4 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 4 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 4 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 4 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Sequence” mean?
options: A list of numbers following a rule
One number in a sequence
How to get from one term to the next
Constant add/subtract / multiply or divide
questions.answer: A list of numbers following a rule
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sequence means A list of numbers following a rule.

questions.question: What does “Term” mean?
options: One number in a sequence
How to get from one term to the next
Constant add/subtract / multiply or divide
A number times itself: 1, 4, 9, 16 …
questions.answer: One number in a sequence
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Term means One number in a sequence.

questions.question: What does “Analogue clock” mean?
options: A round-faced clock with moving hands
A clock that shows the time in numbers
Before midday / after midday
Hours counted 0 to 23, no AM or PM
questions.answer: A round-faced clock with moving hands
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Analogue clock means A round-faced clock with moving hands.

questions.question: What does “Digital clock” mean?
options: A clock that shows the time in numbers
Before midday / after midday
Hours counted 0 to 23, no AM or PM
How long there is between two times
questions.answer: A clock that shows the time in numbers
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Digital clock means A clock that shows the time in numbers.

questions.question: What does “Sum” mean?
options: The answer to an addition
The answer to a subtraction
An operation that undoes another
Exchanging between place-value columns (carry / borrow)
questions.answer: The answer to an addition
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sum means The answer to an addition.
questions.unitTitle: Addition and Subtraction of Whole Numbers

questions.question: What does “Difference” mean?
options: The answer to a subtraction
An operation that undoes another
Exchanging between place-value columns (carry / borrow)
A sensible answer found by rounding
questions.answer: The answer to a subtraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Difference means The answer to a subtraction.
questions.unitTitle: Addition and Subtraction of Whole Numbers

questions.question: What does “Probability” mean?
options: How likely an event is to happen
One possible result of an experiment
Something that might happen
Sure to happen / cannot happen
questions.answer: How likely an event is to happen
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Probability means How likely an event is to happen.

questions.question: What does “Outcome” mean?
options: One possible result of an experiment
Something that might happen
Sure to happen / cannot happen
Equally likely to happen or not (50%)
questions.answer: One possible result of an experiment
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Outcome means One possible result of an experiment.

questions.question: What does “Factor” mean?
options: A number that divides another exactly
The result of a times-table step
The answer to a multiplication
Two factors that multiply to the number
questions.answer: A number that divides another exactly
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Factor means A number that divides another exactly.

questions.question: What does “Multiple” mean?
options: The result of a times-table step
The answer to a multiplication
Two factors that multiply to the number
Objects in equal rows and columns
questions.answer: The result of a times-table step
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Multiple means The result of a times-table step.

questions.question: What does “Polygon” mean?
options: A closed shape made of straight sides
A corner where two sides meet
All sides and angles equal / not all equal
Any 4-sided polygon
questions.answer: A closed shape made of straight sides
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Polygon means A closed shape made of straight sides.

questions.question: What does “Vertex” mean?
options: A corner where two sides meet
All sides and angles equal / not all equal
Any 4-sided polygon
Shapes fitting with no gaps or overlaps
questions.answer: A corner where two sides meet
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Vertex means A corner where two sides meet.

questions.question: What does “Fraction” mean?
options: A number for equal parts of a whole or group
The top number – how many parts you have
The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
Different fractions worth the same amount
questions.answer: A number for equal parts of a whole or group
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Fraction means A number for equal parts of a whole or group.

questions.question: What does “Numerator” mean?
options: The top number – how many parts you have
The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
Different fractions worth the same amount
A fraction reduced by dividing top and bottom
questions.answer: The top number – how many parts you have
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator means The top number – how many parts you have.

questions.question: What does “Angle” mean?
options: The amount of turn between two arms
The point where the two arms meet
The two straight lines forming the angle
Exactly 90°; a square corner
questions.answer: The amount of turn between two arms
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Angle means The amount of turn between two arms.

questions.question: What does “Vertex” mean?
options: The point where the two arms meet
The two straight lines forming the angle
Exactly 90°; a square corner
Less than 90°
questions.answer: The point where the two arms meet
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Vertex means The point where the two arms meet.

questions.question: What does “Compare” mean?
options: Decide which number is larger, smaller or equal
Smallest-to-largest / largest-to-smallest order
Change a number to a nearby, simpler number
A sensible answer found using rounded numbers
questions.answer: Decide which number is larger, smaller or equal
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Compare means Decide which number is larger, smaller or equal.

questions.question: What does “Ascending / Descending” mean?
options: Smallest-to-largest / largest-to-smallest order
Change a number to a nearby, simpler number
A sensible answer found using rounded numbers
The number being divided (the total)
questions.answer: Smallest-to-largest / largest-to-smallest order
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Ascending / Descending means Smallest-to-largest / largest-to-smallest order.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information collected to answer a question
A question answered by collecting data, with varied answers
A counting line, grouped in fives
How many times a value appears
questions.answer: Information collected to answer a question
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information collected to answer a question.

questions.question: What does “Statistical question” mean?
options: A question answered by collecting data, with varied answers
A counting line, grouped in fives
How many times a value appears
A table of categories and their counts
questions.answer: A question answered by collecting data, with varied answers
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Statistical question means A question answered by collecting data, with varied answers.

questions.question: What does “Numerator” mean?
options: The top number of a fraction
The bottom number of a fraction
Different-looking fractions of equal value
A shared denominator for comparing
questions.answer: The top number of a fraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator means The top number of a fraction.

questions.question: What does “Denominator” mean?
options: The bottom number of a fraction
Different-looking fractions of equal value
A shared denominator for comparing
A number out of 100, written with %
questions.answer: The bottom number of a fraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Denominator means The bottom number of a fraction.

questions.question: What does “3D shape” mean?
options: A solid with length, width and height
A flat or curved surface of a solid
The straight line where two faces meet
A corner point where edges meet
questions.answer: A solid with length, width and height
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: 3D shape means A solid with length, width and height.

questions.question: What does “Face” mean?
options: A flat or curved surface of a solid
The straight line where two faces meet
A corner point where edges meet
A solid with flat faces only
questions.answer: A flat or curved surface of a solid
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Face means A flat or curved surface of a solid.

questions.question: What does “Sum” mean?
options: The answer to an addition
The answer to a subtraction
Exchanging between place values (carry / borrow)
An approximate answer found by rounding
questions.answer: The answer to an addition
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sum means The answer to an addition.

questions.question: What does “Difference” mean?
options: The answer to a subtraction
Exchanging between place values (carry / borrow)
An approximate answer found by rounding
The opposite operation, used to check
questions.answer: The answer to a subtraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Difference means The answer to a subtraction.

questions.question: What does “Perimeter” mean?
options: The distance all the way around a shape
The amount of flat space inside a shape
A length unit: mm, cm, m, km
An area unit: mm², cm², m², km²
questions.answer: The distance all the way around a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Perimeter means The distance all the way around a shape.

questions.question: What does “Area” mean?
options: The amount of flat space inside a shape
A length unit: mm, cm, m, km
An area unit: mm², cm², m², km²
Four sides, four right angles, opposite sides equal
questions.answer: The amount of flat space inside a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Area means The amount of flat space inside a shape.

questions.question: What does “Negative number” mean?
options: A number less than zero (−1, −2, −3)
Divides exactly into a number, no remainder
Result of multiplying by a whole number
questions.answer: A number less than zero (−1, −2, −3)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Negative number means A number less than zero (−1, −2, −3).

questions.question: What does “Odd / Even” mean?
options: Divides exactly into a number, no remainder
Result of multiplying by a whole number
A number times itself (1, 4, 9, 16 …)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Odd / Even means Ends in 1,3,5,7,9 / ends in 0,2,4,6,8.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information collected to answer a question
A grid sorting items by two yes/no properties
Overlapping circles showing how groups relate
The overlap for items with both properties
questions.answer: Information collected to answer a question
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information collected to answer a question.

questions.question: What does “Carroll diagram” mean?
options: A grid sorting items by two yes/no properties
Overlapping circles showing how groups relate
The overlap for items with both properties
A display using symbols, each worth a set amount
questions.answer: A grid sorting items by two yes/no properties
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Carroll diagram means A grid sorting items by two yes/no properties.

questions.question: What does “Factor” mean?
options: A number that is multiplied by another
The result of a multiplication
The number being divided
The number you divide by
questions.answer: A number that is multiplied by another
questions.hint: Use the Unit 17 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Factor means A number that is multiplied by another.

questions.question: What does “Product” mean?
options: The result of a multiplication
The number being divided
The number you divide by
The result of a division (not the remainder)
questions.answer: The result of a multiplication
questions.hint: Use the Unit 17 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Product means The result of a multiplication.

questions.question: What does “Coordinates” mean?
options: A pair of numbers naming a position (x, y)
The horizontal / vertical line on a grid
Where the axes meet: (0, 0)
A star showing all eight directions
questions.answer: A pair of numbers naming a position (x, y)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 18 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Coordinates means A pair of numbers naming a position (x, y).

questions.question: What does “x-axis / y-axis” mean?
options: The horizontal / vertical line on a grid
Where the axes meet: (0, 0)
A star showing all eight directions
A flip of a shape over a mirror line
questions.answer: The horizontal / vertical line on a grid
questions.hint: Use the Unit 18 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: x-axis / y-axis means The horizontal / vertical line on a grid.
